title: Vintage Steam Locomotive Machinery at the Rustic Andean Border Hotel in Puerto Frías Neuquén
description: Puerto Frías, Neuquén: An imposing, century-old steam-powered tractor stands as a silent sentinel on the manicured lawns of this remote mountain lodge. The machine’s oxidized iron wheels and stark, vertical smokestack create a compelling industrial silhouette against the vibrant, verdant backdrop of the Andean temperate rainforest. Captured under the crisp, high-altitude light of a clear day, the image utilizes a sharp depth of field to emphasize the intricate gears and rusted textures of the locomotive while softly blurring the sprawling wooden structure of the hotel behind it. The architecture, reminiscent of alpine chalets, harmonizes with the surrounding jagged peaks and dense woodlands that define this historic transit point between Argentina and Chile. This machinery serves as a tangible artifact of the early twentieth-century engineering efforts that first opened these rugged, inaccessible frontier corridors to intrepid travelers. The juxtaposition of man-made heritage against the backdrop of ancient, glaciated mountains encapsulates the resilient spirit of Patagonian colonization and tourism development. Visitors passing through this region are invited to pause and reflect on the mechanical ingenuity that once powered the transport of timber and supplies through these challenging, isolated Andean passes. This scene captures the essential interplay between historical preservation and the awe-inspiring geography that continues to draw explorers to the Cruce Andino today. This image is a collaborative production by Samuel Jeffery and Audrey Bergner for Project 23.

json_ld.description: A vintage steam locomotive sits on the grounds of a mountain hotel near the Andes.
